title: Mira’s Pillow Fight
cover_prompt: A small girl with wild curls, half-asleep, hugging a plump pillow in a cozy moonlit bedroom. The pillow glows softly, and her angry frown melts into a tired smile as fireflies drift near the window.
lyrics:
[verse]
What a day, what a day
Toss and turn, shout and play
Pillow’s warm but I won’t stay
Kick the sheets and run away

[chorus]
Oh, the day is done
Oh, the day is done
Lay your head, my little one
And the day is done

[verse]
Tripped my toe, spilled my cup
Sister took the toy truck up
Daddy said to clean my plate
I just threw it at the gate

[chorus]
Oh, the day is done
Oh, the day is done
Lay your head, my little one
And the day is done

[verse]
Now the moon is soft and low
Pillow holds me, warm and slow
Angry thoughts begin to fade
In the dark my bed’s remade

[chorus]
Oh, the day is done
Oh, the day is done
Lay your head, my little one
And the day is done
style_prompt: Intimate domestic lullaby, warm female vocal, soft acoustic guitar, 64 BPM, cozy, familiar, conversational, almost speaking melodically, not performative, kitchen-to-bedroom energy, end of day warmth, soft piano accompaniment

character.name: Mira
character.identity: A fiery little girl who struggles to let go of her big feelings at bedtime.
character.special: She turns her pillow into a friend who whispers the day is over.

about: A lullaby for children who fight sleep with frustration, acknowledging their anger while gently guiding them toward closure. The repetition of "the day is done" acts as a soothing mantra, helping little ones release the day’s chaos and settle into rest.
